verses.fhb_text: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
verses.decision_notes: Opening of the pericope of the adulteress (7:53–8:11); NIV/NLT/ESV majority retains it, so FHB includes it (FHB-8.5-1). Textual-variant note routes to Tier 2, not the body.

verses.fhb_text: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him, and he sat down and taught them.
verses.decision_notes: Narrative scene-setting; natural English cadence.

verses.fhb_text: The scribes and Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery and placed her in the middle,
verses.decision_notes: Straightforward narrative.

verses.fhb_text: they said to him, "Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.
verses.decision_notes: "Teacher" (Didaskale) preserved as the vocative address (FHB-1.2-26).

verses.fhb_text: Now in the Law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. So what do you say?"
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.

verses.fhb_text: They said this to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. But Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
verses.decision_notes: Names Jesus for clarity in a multi-actor scene (FHB-9.13-1).

verses.fhb_text: And as they kept on questioning him, he stood up and said to them, "Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone at her."
verses.decision_notes: Famous saying rendered in plain modern English.
jesus_words: "Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone at her."

verses.fhb_text: And once more he bent down and wrote on the ground.
verses.decision_notes: Routine narrative.

verses.fhb_text: But when they heard it, they went away one by one, beginning with the older ones, until Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus named for clarity.

verses.fhb_text: Jesus stood up and said to her, "Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"
verses.decision_notes: Direct dialogue preserved.
jesus_words: "Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"

verses.fhb_text: She said, "No one, Lord." And Jesus said, "Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on sin no more."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The woman addresses Jesus, and he releases her without condemnation.

Renderings:
• "Lord" — Kurie addressed to Jesus → "Lord" per FHB-1.2-20 (NLT/ESV tiebreaker reading)
• "sin no more" — the Father's invitation away from sin (FHB-2.4.5-1)
jesus_words: "Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on sin no more."

verses.fhb_text: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Predicate "I am" statement — Jesus naming himself the light.

Renderings:
• "I am the light of the world" — predicate egō eimi + metaphor → normal lowercase (FHB-1.2-6), not the absolute divine-name "I AM"
jesus_words: "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life."

verses.fhb_text: So the Pharisees said to him, "You are bearing witness about yourself; your testimony is not true."
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.

verses.fhb_text: Jesus answered, "Even if I bear witness about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going; but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
verses.decision_notes: "Answered" used for a genuine response to the Pharisees' accusation (FHB-9.14-1).
jesus_words: "Even if I bear witness about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going; but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.

verses.fhb_text: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
verses.decision_notes: Parallel clauses kept with a semicolon (FHB-9.4-2).
jesus_words: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.

verses.fhb_text: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus distinguishes himself from the Father who sent him.

Renderings:
• "the Father who sent me" — Jesus speaking of his sending Father; retains "the Father" per his Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.

verses.fhb_text: In your Law it is written that the testimony of two people is true.
verses.decision_notes: "Two people" (dyo anthrōpōn) in natural English rather than the calque "two men" (FHB-7.12-1).
jesus_words: In your Law it is written that the testimony of two people is true.

verses.fhb_text: I am the one who bears witness about myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness about me."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The double witness — Jesus and the Father.

Renderings:
• "I am the one who bears witness" — predicate egō eimi, lowercase (FHB-1.2-6)
• "the Father who sent me" — Jesus's Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: I am the one who bears witness about myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness about me."

verses.fhb_text: So they said to him, "Where is your Father?" Jesus answered, "You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: To know Jesus is to know the Father.

Renderings:
• "your Father" / "my Father" — preserved as spoken; the crowd's question and Jesus's Person-relational answer (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: "You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also."

verses.fhb_text: These words he spoke in the treasury, as he taught in the temple; but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
verses.decision_notes: Narrative aside; semicolon links the parallel clauses.

verses.fhb_text: So he said to them again, "I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come."
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.
jesus_words: "I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come."

verses.fhb_text: So the Jews said, "Will he kill himself, since he says, 'Where I am going, you cannot come'?"
verses.decision_notes: Nested quotation preserved.

verses.fhb_text: He said to them, "You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
verses.decision_notes: Antithetical parallelism kept with semicolons (FHB-9.4-2). "I am from above" is locative, not the divine-name claim.
jesus_words: "You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.

verses.fhb_text: I told you that you would die in your sins, for unless you believe that I AM, you will die in your sins."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: First absolute "I AM" in the chapter — the divine-name claim.

Renderings:
• "I AM" — absolute egō eimi with no predicate; rendered in caps to signal the divine name (FHB-1.2-6). The interpretive boundary here is debated; rendered absolute with a chapter-level footnote.

Greek: egō eimi ("I am") → "I AM"
verses.footnote: Jesus claims the divine name. "I AM" is how our Father identified himself to Moses at the burning bush (Exodus 3:14). The crowd understood the claim later in this chapter and tried to stone him for blasphemy (v.59).

sources.net: Thus I told you that you will die in your sins. For unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.”

gemini.reason:
Traceback (most recent call last):

    main()
  File "/home/deploy/bin/gemini-text.py", line 157, in main

layer3.rationale: The Greek egō eimi is absolute (no predicate), and rendering it as the divine-name claim "I AM" is a well-supported, mainstream reading of John 8:24; FHB committed to it under FHB-1.2-6 and acknowledged the genuine interpretive debate with a chapter-level footnote, which is exactly what the rules authorize for an ambiguous source.
layer3.source_basis: Greek text (absolute egō eimi) plus the NET/NIV/ESV "I am he" variant; the divergence is the well-known interpretive debate, not a meaning error.

jesus_words: I told you that you would die in your sins, for unless you believe that I AM, you will die in your sins."

verses.fhb_text: So they said to him, "Who are you?" Jesus said to them, "Just what I have been telling you from the beginning.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus named for clarity.
jesus_words: "Just what I have been telling you from the beginning.

verses.fhb_text: I have much to say about you and much to judge, but he who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus declares what he hears from the Father who sent him.

Renderings:
• "he who sent me" — the Father, referenced obliquely by Jesus (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: I have much to say about you and much to judge, but he who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him."

verses.fhb_text: They did not understand that he had been speaking to them about the Father.
verses.decision_notes: Narrator clarifies the antecedent as the Father.

verses.fhb_text: So Jesus said to them, "When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I AM, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just as the Father taught me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Second absolute "I AM"; the Son does only what the Father teaches.

Renderings:
• "I AM" — absolute egō eimi, caps (FHB-1.2-6); footnote economy: established at v.24, not repeated
• "the Father taught me" — Jesus's Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: "When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I AM, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just as the Father taught me.

verses.fhb_text: And he who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's unbroken presence with the Son.

Renderings:
• "he who sent me" — the Father (FHB-4.0.3-1)
jesus_words: And he who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him."

verses.fhb_text: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
verses.decision_notes: Narrative summary.

verses.fhb_text: So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed him, "If you remain in my word, you are truly my disciples,
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.
jesus_words: "If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples,

verses.fhb_text: and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."
verses.decision_notes: Well-known saying in plain English.
jesus_words: and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."

verses.fhb_text: They answered him, "We are offspring of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone. How is it that you say, 'You will become free'?"
verses.decision_notes: "Offspring of Abraham" (sperma) — lineage claim; preserved as offspring here since the emphasis is genealogical descent (FHB-5.10-1).

verses.fhb_text: Jesus answered them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin.
verses.decision_notes: "Truly, truly" renders the doubled amēn that opens Jesus's weighty sayings (consensus form per FHB-8.4-1).
jesus_words: "Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin.

verses.fhb_text: The slave does not remain in the house forever; the son remains forever.
verses.decision_notes: Contrast preserved with a semicolon.
jesus_words: The slave does not remain in the house forever; the son remains forever.

verses.fhb_text: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
verses.decision_notes: "the Son" — the Son's freeing work emphasizes the filial relationship (FHB-7.2.2-1).
jesus_words: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

verses.fhb_text: I know that you are offspring of Abraham; yet you seek to kill me because there is no room in you for my word.
verses.decision_notes: "Offspring of Abraham" — genealogical sense preserved.
jesus_words: I know that you are offspring of Abraham; yet you seek to kill me because my word finds no place in you.

verses.fhb_text: I speak of what I have seen with my Father, and you do what you have heard from your father."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Two fatherhoods set against each other.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — Jesus's Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1); "your father" lowercase, anticipating v.44's identification
jesus_words: I speak of what I have seen with my Father, and you do what you have heard from your father."

verses.fhb_text: They answered him, "Abraham is our father." Jesus said to them, "If you were Abraham's children, you would be doing the works Abraham did,
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue; "father" here is the human patriarch Abraham, lowercase.
jesus_words: "If you were Abraham's children, you would be doing the works Abraham did,

verses.fhb_text: but now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from my Father. This is not what Abraham did.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus speaks the truth he received from the Father.

Renderings:
• "from my Father" — Theos as source distinguished from the Son; resolves to the Father, in Jesus's voice as "my Father" (FHB-4.3.1-1, FHB-1.2-1 grammatical agreement)

Greek: para tou theou ("from God") → "from my Father"
jesus_words: but now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from my Father. This is not what Abraham did.

verses.fhb_text: You are doing the works your father did." They said to him, "We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father—our Father himself."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The crowd claims God as their one Father.

Renderings:
• "one Father—God himself" — the Jews' own claim (theos in their speech); preserved as "God" per the speaker context, not resolved (FHB-1.2-1 dialogue, FHB-4.3.3-1)

verses.fhb_text: Jesus said to them, "If our Father were your Father, you would love me, for I came from our Father and I am here. I came not on my own, but he sent me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus tests their claim — true children of the Father love the Son.

Renderings:
• "If God were your Father" — predicative use within Jesus's conditional argument; "God" retained so the logic holds (FHB-4.3.3-1)
• "I came from God" — kept parallel with the predicative clause; preserved as "God"
jesus_words: "If our Father were your Father, you would love me, for I came from our Father and I am here. I came not on my own, but he sent me.

verses.fhb_text: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot bear to hear my word.
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.
jesus_words: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot bear to hear my word.

verses.fhb_text: You are of your father the devil, and you want to carry out your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus names the devil as a fallen rebel and the source of the lie.

Renderings:
• "the father of lies" — preserved; the devil is the one who has rejected the truth from the beginning (FHB-2.9-1). Pronouns for the devil lowercase.
verses.footnote: Jesus exposes the foundational lie of Eden by name. The serpent who told Eve our Father was withholding good from her — the serpent who had rejected the Father himself — is the original liar, the father of every lie that paints our Father as distant. Those who reject the Son share that father, not Abraham, not God.
jesus_words: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.

verses.fhb_text: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.
jesus_words: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.

verses.fhb_text: Which one of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
verses.decision_notes: Rhetorical questions; periods/question marks, no added exclamation (FHB-9.15-1).
jesus_words: Which one of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

verses.fhb_text: Whoever belongs to our Father hears his words. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to our Father."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Belonging to the Father is what opens the ear to his words.

Renderings:
• "belongs to our Father" / "words of our Father" — "of God" (ek tou theou) distinguished from the Son resolves to "our Father" (FHB-1.2-1 X-of-God, FHB-4.3.1-1)

Greek: ho ōn ek tou theou ("the one who is of God") → "whoever belongs to our Father"
jesus_words: Whoever belongs to our Father hears the words of our Father. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to our Father."

verses.fhb_text: The Jews answered him, "Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?"
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.

verses.fhb_text: Jesus answered, "I do not have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
verses.decision_notes: "my Father" — Jesus's Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1).
jesus_words: "I do not have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.

verses.fhb_text: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it, and he is the judge.
verses.decision_notes: The Father is the implied glory-seeker and judge; pronoun left as Jesus phrases it.
jesus_words: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it, and he is the judge.

verses.fhb_text: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death."
verses.decision_notes: "Truly, truly" opens the weighty saying (consensus form).
jesus_words: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death."

verses.fhb_text: The Jews said to him, "Now we know that you have a demon! Abraham died, as did the prophets, yet you say, 'If anyone keeps my word, he will never taste death.'
verses.decision_notes: Exclamation retained — a genuine shout of accusation (FHB-9.15-1).

verses.fhb_text: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? And the prophets died! Who do you make yourself out to be?"
verses.decision_notes: "our father Abraham" — human patriarch, lowercase. Exclamation kept for the incredulous outburst.

verses.fhb_text: Jesus answered, "If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, 'He is our Father.'
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father glorifies the Son; the crowd claims that same God.

Renderings:
• "my Father who glorifies me" — Jesus's Person-relation (FHB-4.0.3-1)
• "He is our God" — the crowd's own confession, preserved as spoken (FHB-1.2-1 dialogue)
jesus_words: "If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, 'He is our Father.'

verses.fhb_text: But you have not known him. I know him. If I were to say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you; but I do know him, and I keep his word.
verses.decision_notes: Pronouns refer to the Father; left as Jesus phrases them. Semicolon links the contrast.
jesus_words: But you have not known him. I know him. If I were to say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you; but I do know him, and I keep his word.

verses.fhb_text: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad."
verses.decision_notes: "Your father Abraham" — human patriarch, lowercase.
jesus_words: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad."

verses.fhb_text: So the Jews said to him, "You are not yet fifty years old, and you have seen Abraham?"
verses.decision_notes: Routine dialogue.

verses.fhb_text: Jesus said to them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I AM."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The unambiguous absolute "I AM" — Jesus's eternal self-existence.

Renderings:
• "I AM" — absolute egō eimi, caps for the divine name (FHB-1.2-6); footnote established at v.24, not repeated here per once-per-book economy

Greek: prin Abraam genesthai egō eimi ("before Abraham came to be, I am") → "before Abraham was, I AM"
verses.footnote: "I AM" echoes the divine name God spoke to Moses in Exodus 3:14 — a claim to be God himself, which is exactly why his listeners immediately reached for stones.
jesus_words: "Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I AM."

verses.fhb_text: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
verses.decision_notes: The crowd's reaction confirms they understood the "I AM" of v.58 as a divine-name claim worthy (to them) of stoning.

chapter_notes: John 8 opens with the pericope of the adulteress (7:53–8:11), retained in the body per FHB-8.5-1 (NIV/NLT/ESV majority retains; textual note routes to Tier 2). The chapter's theological spine is Jesus's escalating self-revelation, climaxing in three absolute 'I AM' claims (vv.24, 28, 58 — FHB-1.2-6) rendered in caps to signal the divine name of Exodus 3:14; the establishing footnote sits at the first occurrence (v.24). Predicate 'I am the light of the world' (v.12) stays lowercase. Father references throughout are governed by Jesus-as-speaker grammatical agreement (FHB-1.2-1 / FHB-4.0.3-1): 'my Father' where uniquely his, 'the Father who sent me' where distinguished. Theos distinguished from the Son resolves to 'our Father' (v.40, v.47); predicative theos within Jesus's conditional argument (v.42) and the crowd's own claims (vv.41, 54) are preserved as 'God' per dialogue/speaker context.
flags_summary: Layer 3 fidelity self-check: 1 verse(s) checked — 1 confirmed, 0 revised (applied), 0 suggested, 0 unresolved [independent reviewer model unavailable this run]

headings.text: I Am the Light of the World
